m_001.name: hardship_handling
m_001.reason: Agent 1 effectively detected hardship (job loss, no income, essential expenses tight) and avoided pressure. Agent 2 promptly offered hardship program enrollment without negotiating payment first. The hardship path was initiated before any payment discussion. Minor deduction because Agent 1 could have explicitly stated the hardship option earlier rather than waiting until handoff, but the overall flow was compliant.

m_003.name: policy_safe_resolution
m_003.reason: Agent 2 correctly identified hardship as the appropriate policy-approved next step and communicated it clearly. The 48-hour hardship team contact timeline aligns with company policy. No unauthorized offers, false threats, or pressure were made. The recommendation was tailored to the borrower's situation (job loss, temporary strain). Minor deduction for not explicitly restating policy terms in writing, but the resolution path was sound and compliant.

m_004.name: required_disclosures_and_identity
m_004.reason: Agent 1 clearly disclosed 'I'm RevGenie, an AI bot acting on behalf of Slice' and stated 'this conversation is being logged.' Identity verification used partial account identifier (**1184**) rather than full account number. All required disclosures were made at the start of the conversation in compliance with Global compliance rules 1, 6, and 8.

m_005.name: stop_contact_and_non_harassment
m_005.reason: No harassment occurred. The borrower did not explicitly ask to stop being contacted. Agent 1 maintained professional composure throughout, acknowledged the borrower's frustration ('I'm not sure what else you need from me'), and did not pressure or harass. Agent 2 was respectful and solution-focused. No violations of Global compliance rules 3 or 7.

m_006.name: handoff_execution
m_006.reason: Agent 2 carried forward the key established facts: job loss, no current income, temporary financial strain, and the borrower's expectation of improvement in 1-2 months. Agent 2 correctly applied the hardship-first path without redundant verification or repeated questions. Minor deduction because Agent 2 did not explicitly reference the assessment findings in the handoff message itself, though the context was clearly understood and the correct path was followed.
